_meta.title: Phylogeographic language-spread model for Australian languages
_meta.purpose: Dataset for an animated wind-map/particle-flow of how language lineages spread across Australia. Particles flow along 'edges' (ancestor->descendant) using from/to locations and (where dated) from/to ages.
_meta.READ_FIRST: See 'caveats'. A spreading language family is NOT people arriving; Aboriginal presence ~65,000 yr, the Pama-Nyungan LINGUISTIC spread ~5,600 yr.

caveats:
SCIENTIFIC-HONESTY CAVEATS (read before using this dataset).
1. A LANGUAGE FAMILY SPREADING IS NOT PEOPLE ARRIVING. Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander presence in Australia is on the order of 65,000 years. The Pama-Nyungan expansion modelled here is a much more recent LINGUISTIC event (root age ~5,600 years BP; 95% HPD ~4,500-7,000 BP) that spread across an ALREADY, LONG-POPULATED continent. Every 'spread' edge is the movement of a LANGUAGE/lineage through existing communities via contact, shift and social transmission - it is NOT a migration of first peoples and NOT a population-replacement of the continent. Do not narrate these arrows as 'people arriving'.
2. TOPOLOGY vs GEOGRAPHY/TIME. Tree TOPOLOGY (who is related to whom, nesting of subgroups) is comparatively well supported. Geographic DIFFUSION PATHS and DATES are INFERRED, MODEL-DEPENDENT and UNCERTAIN. Treat every location and every date as a hypothesis with an interval, not a fact. Each node/edge carries a 'confidence' block making this explicit.
3. WHAT IS MEASURED vs MODELLED. Leaf (present-day language) coordinates are observed reference points. Ancestral internal-node locations are either (a) RECONSTRUCTED by the Bouckaert et al. 2018 Bayesian relaxed-random-walk phylogeographic model (with 80% HPD regions in the source tree), or (b) a transparent CENTROID (mean of descendant leaf coordinates) where no published reconstruction exists. The 'placement_method' field states which, per node. Centroids are a drawing convenience, NOT a claim about where an ancestral language was actually spoken.
4. DATES. Real calendar dates (years before present) with 95% HPD intervals are attached ONLY to Pama-Nyungan nodes taken from / bridged to the Bouckaert et al. 2018 time-calibrated tree. All other nodes (non-Pama-Nyungan families, and Pama-Nyungan Glottolog subgroups that do not exactly match a dated clade) carry time_basis='topology_only': their 'depth' is CLASSIFICATION RANK, i.e. RELATIVE tree structure, NOT calendar time. No dates were fabricated for them.
5. NON-PAMA-NYUNGAN FAMILIES. The ~25+ non-Pama-Nyungan families of the north (Arnhem Land, the Top End, the Kimberley) are far older linguistic groupings than the Pama-Nyungan spread and are NOT time-calibrated here. Their internal structure is shown from Glottolog classification with centroid placement only. Their comparative antiquity and northern concentration are exactly why the recent Pama-Nyungan expansion stands out - but this dataset does NOT date them.
6. THE ORIGIN ANCHOR. The Pama-Nyungan root is placed at the Gulf Plains / Gulf of Carpentaria point published by Bouckaert et al. 2018 (location-median of their summary-tree root), NOT at a naive centroid of all Pama-Nyungan languages. This is a deliberate, cited modelling choice; the origin itself remains a scientific hypothesis under active debate.
7. SOURCES & LICENSING. Topology/dates/locations for Pama-Nyungan derive from Bouckaert, Bowern & Atkinson 2018 as standardised by the Phlorest project (CLDF, CC-BY-4.0). Classification for all families is Glottolog 5.3 (CC-BY-4.0). See SOURCES.md for URLs, licences and checksums. Respect attribution; this is Indigenous linguistic heritage - represent it carefully and do not overstate certainty.

sources.citation: Bouckaert, R. R., Bowern, C. & Atkinson, Q. D. (2018). The origin and expansion of Pama-Nyungan languages across Australia. Nature Ecology & Evolution 2, 741-749.
sources.used_for: Pama-Nyungan time-calibrated topology, node ages (years BP + 95% HPD), and reconstructed ancestral locations (relaxed random walk); Gulf-of-Carpentaria origin anchor.
sources.data_obtained_via: Phlorest CLDF standardisation, github.com/phlorest/bouckaert_et_al2018 (summary.trees, languages.csv). Zenodo record 10.5281/zenodo.17581679; original data Zenodo record 1320471.
sources.license: CC-BY-4.0 (Phlorest CLDF distribution, dc:license in Generic-metadata.json). NOTE: the D-PLACE landing page labels the derived phylogeny CC-BY-NC-4.0 - see SOURCES.md; treat commercial reuse conservatively and attribute the authors.

sources.citation: Bowern, C. & Atkinson, Q. (2012). Computational phylogenetics and the internal structure of Pama-Nyungan. Language 88(4), 817-845.
sources.used_for: Cross-reference Pama-Nyungan phylogeny (summary tree fetched; not the primary backbone here).

sources.citation: Hammarstrom, H., Forkel, R., Haspelmath, M. & Bank, S. (2024). Glottolog 5.3. Leipzig: MPI for Evolutionary Anthropology.
sources.used_for: Classification backbone (family -> subgroup -> language) for ALL Australian languoids; leaf coordinates.
sources.data_obtained_via: Local Glottolog 5.3 CLDF on mount (glottolog/cldf) via the project registry.

sources.citation: Project master registry of Australian Aboriginal & Torres Strait Islander languoids (build_registry.py), joining Glottolog 5.3, Grambank v1.0.3, WALS, AIATSIS AUSTLANG.
sources.used_for: Leaf set, family_chain classification paths, coordinates.

origin.description: Published Gulf-of-Carpentaria / Gulf Plains origin of the Pama-Nyungan expansion.
origin.note: Root of the Bouckaert et al. 2018 Bayesian phylogeographic summary tree; Gulf Plains region, mid-Holocene. Point is the model's location-median at the root.

glottolog.description: Classification topology for ALL Australian families; internal nodes placed by centroid; time = topology depth (unless bridged to a dated Bouckaert clade).
glottolog.confidence: topology=high; geography=modeled(centroid); time=topology_only (except bridged PN nodes).

bouckaert2018.description: Time-calibrated Bayesian phylogeographic tree for Pama-Nyungan (306 taxa). Per-node reconstructed ancestral location + age in years BP. The gold animation layer.
bouckaert2018.confidence: topology=high(published); geography=modeled_reconstructed(80% HPD in source); time=modeled_dated(95% HPD).

bouckaert2018_backbone.interpolation_note: A minority of internal nodes carried no BEAST location/age annotation (typically very low posterior clades). Their location = children centroid and age = monotonic interpolation between parent and children; each such node is flagged location_interpolated / age_interpolated with confidence downgraded.

clade_bridge.note: Glottolog internal nodes whose descendant-Glottocode set exactly equals a Bouckaert clade inherit that clade's reconstructed location + age. Topology differences between Glottolog and the Bouckaert Bayesian tree limit the number of exact matches.

stats.real_dates_source: Bouckaert, Bowern & Atkinson 2018 (BEAST time-calibrated phylogeographic summary tree, phlorest CLDF, CC-BY-4.0)

guma1253.label: Gumatj (a Yolngu variety; in the dated Bouckaert 2018 sample)

djam1256.label: Djambarrpuyngu (a Yolngu variety; Glottolog-only, not in the 306-taxon dated sample)

nodes.name: Far North Queensland Indigenous Sign Language
Ngarla on the Ashburton river south of the Hamersley range
